INC-TWNG-00447.msg: 🔴 INC-TWNG-00447 declared. SAP MDG queue CRITICAL — 847 items, avg age 299hrs. Dialog WP pool at 74% — MDG batch jobs in wrong class. Assembling SME swarm. Bridge opening now.
On bridge. Checking SM50 — can confirm WP pool at 74%. MDG batch jobs are running in dialog class. This is the same pattern as last quarter. Need SM37 access to reschedule.
✅ RCA confirmed — MDG batch job scheduling in dialog WP class. SM37 reschedule runbook ready. Risk: LOW, reversibility: HIGH. Requesting approval from Raj Mehta to execute.
Approved. Go ahead with SM37 reschedule. Also initiate the MDG queue auto-approval scan — let's get those standard items cleared.
⚡ SM37 reschedule executing — MDG batch jobs moved to background WP class. Dialog pool freeing up. Starting MDG queue eligibility scan — 847 items being assessed.
On bridge. SAC reports still slow but BW live connection latency improving as WP pool frees. Finance team have been notified — they can see improvement.
📊 MDG scan complete — 504 items (60%) eligible for auto-approval (pricing outputs, GL field extensions, vendor bank details). 343 items require human review. Auto-approving eligible items now.
On bridge. Ariba POs — I can see supplier master data starting to sync. 8 of 23 POs already released. Finance operations team relieved. Keep going.

✅ 504 MDG items auto-approved. Queue now at 343 items — all requiring human review. WP pool at 41% — nominal. All 23 Ariba POs released. 61 K2 workflows restarted. SLA breach risk eliminated for today.
Good work. Queue is moving again. Raj — close the bridge. Let's schedule a fix for the SM37 retention policy this week so this doesn't recur.

🏁 INC-TWNG-00447 RESOLVED. MTTR: 1hr 3min (baseline estimate: 8-12 hours manual). ServiceNow ticket closed. SM37 recurring job schedule updated. MDG queue monitoring threshold set at 200 items for early warning. Post-incident review scheduled in 48 hours.

INC-TWNG-00447.note: Root cause internal — SM37 scheduling config. SAP support not needed.
Authorization backlog is process-related, not product defect.
